_meta.comment: Hover-tooltip reference for the in-browser editor. Each entry is one Inside-Macintosh-style signature line + a one-paragraph what-it-does + a 'see also' list of related calls. Coverage is intentionally MVP — the calls the bundled wasm-* samples actually use — and skewed toward the System 7-era Toolbox surface our wasm-cc1 sysroot exposes. Add more entries as samples reach for new APIs.
_meta.source: Signatures and behaviour distilled from Inside Macintosh: Macintosh Toolbox Essentials (1992), Inside Macintosh: Imaging With QuickDraw (1994), Inside Macintosh: Text (1993), and Inside Macintosh: Files (1992). One-sentence summaries are paraphrased for brevity; consult the originals via bitsavers for the full word.

InitGraf.blurb: Initialises QuickDraw's global state. Must be the first Toolbox call in main() — pass &qd.thePort. Wires the QDGlobals (qd.black, qd.white, qd.gray, etc.) and the desktop port.

InitFonts.blurb: Initialises the Font Manager. Call after InitGraf.

InitWindows.blurb: Initialises the Window Manager and paints the desktop.

InitMenus.blurb: Initialises the Menu Manager. Required before GetMenuBar / SetMenuBar.

InitDialogs.blurb: Initialises the Dialog Manager. Pass 0 (NIL) to use the default resume handler.

InitCursor.blurb: Resets the cursor to the arrow. Also used after SetCursor to restore the default.

MoveTo.signature: pascal void MoveTo(short h, short v)
MoveTo.blurb: Sets the pen position for the current GrafPort without drawing. Coordinates are local to the port.

LineTo.signature: pascal void LineTo(short h, short v)
LineTo.blurb: Draws a line from the current pen position to (h, v), and updates the pen position.

DrawString.blurb: Draws a Pascal-string at the current pen position using the port's font/size/style. The string's first byte is its length.

StringWidth.blurb: Returns the pixel width of a Pascal-string in the current font/size. Handy for centring text.

TextFont.blurb: Sets the current font for the port. 0 = system font (Chicago).

TextSize.blurb: Sets the point size for text drawn into the current port. 0 means use the font's default.

TextFace.blurb: Sets the text style (bold, italic, underline, etc.) as a bitmask.

EraseRect.blurb: Fills a rectangle with the port's background pattern (white by default). Used to clear regions before redrawing.

FrameRect.blurb: Draws the outline of a rectangle using the current pen pattern and size.

PaintRect.blurb: Fills a rectangle with the current pen pattern.

FillRect.signature: pascal void FillRect(const Rect* r, const Pattern* pat)
FillRect.blurb: Fills a rectangle with the given pattern. Pass &qd.gray, &qd.ltGray, &qd.dkGray, or a custom 8x8 pattern.

InvertRect.blurb: Inverts every pixel in the rectangle (white↔black). Used for selection highlighting.

FrameOval.blurb: Draws an ellipse fitted to the rectangle.

FillOval.signature: pascal void FillOval(const Rect* r, const Pattern* pat)
FillOval.blurb: Fills an ellipse fitted to the rectangle with the given pattern.

FrameRoundRect.signature: pascal void FrameRoundRect(const Rect* r, short ovalWidth, short ovalHeight)
FrameRoundRect.blurb: Draws the outline of a rounded-corner rectangle. ovalWidth/Height set the corner radius.

InvertRoundRect.signature: pascal void InvertRoundRect(const Rect* r, short ovalWidth, short ovalHeight)
InvertRoundRect.blurb: Inverts a rounded-corner rectangle. Classic Mac press-feedback for buttons: invert on mouseDown, invert again to restore.

CopyBits.signature: pascal void CopyBits(const BitMap* srcBits, const BitMap* dstBits, const Rect* srcRect, const Rect* dstRect, short mode, RgnHandle maskRgn)
CopyBits.blurb: Blits one BitMap region into another. The double-buffer workhorse: draw into an offscreen BitMap, then CopyBits onto the visible window in one shot.

OffsetRect.signature: pascal void OffsetRect(Rect* r, short dh, short dv)
OffsetRect.blurb: Translates a rectangle in-place by (dh, dv).

PtInRect.signature: pascal Boolean PtInRect(Point pt, const Rect* r)
PtInRect.blurb: Returns true if the point lies inside the rectangle. Used for hit-testing hand-drawn buttons.

RGBForeColor.blurb: Sets the foreground colour for the current Color QuickDraw port. Components are 16-bit (0..0xFFFF).

RGBBackColor.blurb: Sets the background colour. Sticks for subsequent EraseRect calls in this port.

GetNewWindow.signature: pascal WindowPtr GetNewWindow(short windowID, void* storage, WindowPtr behind)
GetNewWindow.blurb: Creates a window from a WIND resource. Pass NULL for storage to let the Window Manager allocate, and (WindowPtr)(-1) to put the new window at the front.

NewWindow.signature: pascal WindowPtr NewWindow(void* storage, const Rect* boundsRect, ConstStr255Param title, Boolean visible, short procID, WindowPtr behind, Boolean goAwayFlag, long refCon)
NewWindow.blurb: Creates a window with parameters supplied at runtime, no WIND resource needed.

HideWindow.blurb: Hides a window without disposing it.

DisposeWindow.blurb: Closes a window and releases its Window Manager record.

SetPort.blurb: Sets the current GrafPort. Subsequent QuickDraw calls draw into this port. WindowPtr cast to GrafPtr is the common case.

FindWindow.signature: pascal short FindWindow(Point pt, WindowPtr* whichWindow)
FindWindow.blurb: Returns the part of the screen the point hit (inMenuBar, inContent, inDrag, inGoAway, etc.) and writes the window pointer into *whichWindow.

BeginUpdate.blurb: Restricts the port's clipping to the update region. Always paired with EndUpdate inside an updateEvt handler.

EndUpdate.blurb: Restores the clipping and clears the update region. Pair with BeginUpdate.

DragWindow.signature: pascal void DragWindow(WindowPtr w, Point startPt, const Rect* boundsRect)
DragWindow.blurb: Drags a window around inside boundsRect. Tracks the mouse until release. boundsRect is usually qd.screenBits.bounds with the menubar excluded.

MoveWindow.signature: pascal void MoveWindow(WindowPtr w, short h, short v, Boolean front)
MoveWindow.blurb: Moves a window to (h, v) in global coordinates. Pass true for `front` to bring to front.

TrackGoAway.signature: pascal Boolean TrackGoAway(WindowPtr w, Point startPt)
TrackGoAway.blurb: Highlights the close box and returns true if the user releases inside it. Call from FindWindow's inGoAway branch.

SelectWindow.blurb: Brings a window to the front, sends activate/deactivate events automatically. Use on inContent clicks against any non-front window.

FrontWindow.blurb: Returns the front-most window, or NULL if none.

GetWRefCon.blurb: Reads the 'refCon' (long) stashed in a window's WindowRecord. The Toolbox doesn't use it; it's yours to put per-window state in.

SetWRefCon.signature: pascal void SetWRefCon(WindowPtr w, long data)
SetWRefCon.blurb: Writes a long into the window's refCon slot.

InvalRect.blurb: Marks a rectangle as needing repaint. The Window Manager dispatches an updateEvt later.

GlobalToLocal.blurb: Converts a point from global screen coordinates to the current port's local coordinates.

WaitNextEvent.signature: pascal Boolean WaitNextEvent(short eventMask, EventRecord* event, unsigned long sleep, RgnHandle mouseRgn)
WaitNextEvent.blurb: Pulls the next event from the queue. `sleep` is the max ticks to wait (1/60 s each); 0 means poll. Returns true if a real event came back, false on nullEvent.

TickCount.blurb: Returns ticks since startup (60 per second). The cheap monotonic clock for game loops and timeouts.

GetMouse.blurb: Reads the current mouse position in the current port's local coordinates. Use on nullEvent ticks for region tracking.

StillDown.blurb: Returns true while the mouse button is held down. Inner-loop primitive for drag-to-draw, scrollbar tracking, etc.

GetNewMBar.blurb: Loads an MBAR resource and the MENUs it references. Pair with SetMenuBar + DrawMenuBar.

SetMenuBar.blurb: Installs a menu bar previously obtained from GetMenuBar or GetNewMBar.

DrawMenuBar.blurb: Redraws the menu bar. Call after installing or modifying the menus.

GetMenuHandle.blurb: Returns the MenuHandle for a menu by ID. Used to CheckItem / EnableItem on specific entries.

AppendResMenu.signature: pascal void AppendResMenu(MenuHandle menu, ResType type)
AppendResMenu.blurb: Appends every resource of the given type to a menu. Classic use: AppendResMenu(appleMenu, 'DRVR') to add desk accessories.

MenuSelect.blurb: Tracks a menu pull-down. Returns HiWord(result) = menuID, LoWord(result) = item. Both 0 if the user released outside any item.

MenuKey.blurb: Translates a Cmd-key keystroke to a menu/item pair, same return shape as MenuSelect.

HiliteMenu.blurb: Highlights a menu title. Pass 0 to clear all highlights — what to call after dispatching a MenuSelect/MenuKey result.

CheckItem.signature: pascal void CheckItem(MenuHandle menu, short item, Boolean checked)
CheckItem.blurb: Adds or removes the checkmark on a menu item.

TENew.signature: pascal TEHandle TENew(const Rect* destRect, const Rect* viewRect)
TENew.blurb: Creates a single-style (mono-style) editable text record. destRect is the wrap rectangle, viewRect is the clipping rectangle (commonly the same).

TEDispose.blurb: Releases a TEHandle and its associated storage.

TESetText.signature: pascal void TESetText(const void* text, long length, TEHandle hTE)
TESetText.blurb: Replaces the entire text in a TEHandle. Pass a C buffer + byte count.

TEGetText.blurb: Returns a handle to the current text. Lock with HLock before reading bytes, HUnlock after.

TEKey.signature: pascal void TEKey(short key, TEHandle hTE)
TEKey.blurb: Inserts a key into the TEHandle at the selection. Handles backspace / arrows / delete itself.

TEClick.signature: pascal void TEClick(Point pt, Boolean extend, TEHandle hTE)
TEClick.blurb: Handles a mouse click in the TE field — caret placement, drag-select. `extend` = shift-click.

TESetSelect.signature: pascal void TESetSelect(long selStart, long selEnd, TEHandle hTE)
TESetSelect.blurb: Sets the current selection by character offsets. Use (0x7FFF, 0x7FFF) to move the caret to the end.

TEUpdate.signature: pascal void TEUpdate(const Rect* updateRect, TEHandle hTE)
TEUpdate.blurb: Redraws the TE field. Call from an updateEvt handler between BeginUpdate and EndUpdate.

TEIdle.blurb: Blinks the caret. Call from nullEvent handlers so the cursor doesn't freeze.

TEActivate.blurb: Activates the TE field (shows the caret, draws selection in active style).

TEDeactivate.blurb: Deactivates the TE field (hides the caret, draws selection greyed). Use on background-activate events.

TECut.blurb: Cuts the current selection to the desk scrap.

TECopy.blurb: Copies the current selection to the desk scrap.

TEPaste.blurb: Pastes the desk scrap at the current selection.

TECalText.blurb: Recalculates line breaks after changing the text or the style. Call after txFont/txSize/txFace changes.

StandardGetFile.signature: pascal void StandardGetFile(FileFilterProcPtr fileFilter, short numTypes, ConstSFTypeListPtr typeList, StandardFileReply* reply)
StandardGetFile.blurb: Pops the system Open dialog. Pass a typeList of OSTypes ('TEXT', 'PICT', ...) to filter. Returns the user's choice in `reply` (check reply->sfGood).

StandardPutFile.signature: pascal void StandardPutFile(ConstStr255Param prompt, ConstStr255Param defaultName, StandardFileReply* reply)
StandardPutFile.blurb: Pops the system Save As dialog. `reply->sfReplacing` is true if the user picked an existing file.

FSpCreate.signature: pascal OSErr FSpCreate(const FSSpec* spec, OSType creator, OSType fileType, ScriptCode scriptTag)
FSpCreate.blurb: Creates a new file at the given FSSpec with the given creator + type ('TEXT', 'APPL', etc.). Returns dupFNErr if the file already exists.

FSpOpenDF.signature: pascal OSErr FSpOpenDF(const FSSpec* spec, char permission, short* refNum)
FSpOpenDF.blurb: Opens the data fork of a file. Permission is fsRdPerm, fsWrPerm, or fsRdWrPerm. Returns a refNum to use with FSRead/FSWrite.

FSClose.blurb: Closes a file opened with FSpOpenDF / FSpOpenRF.

FSRead.signature: pascal OSErr FSRead(short refNum, long* count, void* buffPtr)
FSRead.blurb: Reads up to *count bytes into buffPtr. Updates *count with the actual bytes read. Returns eofErr at end-of-file.

FSWrite.signature: pascal OSErr FSWrite(short refNum, long* count, const void* buffPtr)
FSWrite.blurb: Writes *count bytes from buffPtr. Updates *count with the actual bytes written.

GetEOF.signature: pascal OSErr GetEOF(short refNum, long* logEOF)
GetEOF.blurb: Gets the logical end-of-file (the file's size in bytes).

SetEOF.signature: pascal OSErr SetEOF(short refNum, long logEOF)
SetEOF.blurb: Sets the file's logical end-of-file. Call after FSWrite when overwriting a shorter file to truncate the tail.

SysBeep.blurb: Plays the system alert tone. Duration is in ticks (1/60 s). BasiliskII collapses the duration parameter to a fixed-length sample, so vary the count to make the effect audibly different.

NewPtr.blurb: Allocates a non-relocatable block from the application heap. Returns NULL on failure (MemError() for details).

NewHandle.blurb: Allocates a relocatable block. Returns a Handle (Ptr to Ptr). Lock with HLock before dereferencing across Toolbox calls.

HLock.blurb: Locks a handle so the Memory Manager won't move it during compaction.

GetCursor.blurb: Loads a cursor from a CURS resource. Standard system cursors: iBeamCursor, crossCursor (2), watchCursor (4), plusCursor.

SetCursor.blurb: Sets the current cursor. Pass the dereferenced CursHandle: SetCursor(*myCursorHandle).

NewGWorld.signature: pascal QDErr NewGWorld(GWorldPtr* offscreenGWorld, short PixelDepth, const Rect* boundsRect, CTabHandle cTable, GDHandle aGDevice, GWorldFlags flags)
NewGWorld.blurb: Allocates an offscreen graphics world. Switch to it with SetGWorld, draw with normal QuickDraw, then CopyBits the pixmap to your window. Depth 1 = monochrome, 8 = indexed, 16/32 = direct.

DisposeGWorld.blurb: Releases an offscreen GWorld and its pixmap. Call before quitting.

SetGWorld.signature: pascal void SetGWorld(CGrafPtr port, GDHandle gdh)
SetGWorld.blurb: Sets the current Color GrafPort + GDevice. Used to switch drawing target to an offscreen GWorld and back.

GetGWorld.signature: pascal void GetGWorld(CGrafPtr* port, GDHandle* gdh)
GetGWorld.blurb: Reads the current port + GDevice so you can restore them after a SetGWorld.

GetGWorldPixMap.blurb: Returns the offscreen GWorld's pixmap handle. CopyBits accepts `(BitMap*)*pmh` after LockPixels.

LockPixels.blurb: Locks a GWorld's pixmap so its baseAddr stays stable across CopyBits. Returns false if the pixmap was purged.

NumToString.signature: pascal void NumToString(long num, Str255 theString)
NumToString.blurb: Converts a long to a Pascal-string. The classic way to render a number for DrawString.

StringToNum.signature: pascal void StringToNum(ConstStr255Param theString, long* theNum)
StringToNum.blurb: Parses a Pascal-string as a decimal long.

GetDateTime.blurb: Returns seconds since 1904-01-01 in *secs. Pair with SecondsToDate to break out year/month/day/hour/minute/second.

SecondsToDate.signature: pascal void SecondsToDate(unsigned long secs, DateTimeRec* d)
SecondsToDate.blurb: Converts a GetDateTime value into a DateTimeRec with year/month/day/hour/minute/second/dayOfWeek fields.

GetResource.signature: pascal Handle GetResource(ResType theType, short theID)
GetResource.blurb: Loads a resource by 4-char type + numeric ID. Returns a Handle (locked or relocatable depending on the resource's attributes), or NULL if not found. Common types: 'WIND', 'MENU', 'DLOG', 'ICON', 'snd '.

GetIndResource.signature: pascal Handle GetIndResource(ResType theType, short index)
GetIndResource.blurb: Iterates resources of one type by 1-based index. Handy for walking every 'snd ' or 'PICT' in the current resource chain without knowing their IDs.

ReleaseResource.blurb: Tells the Resource Manager you're done with this resource. Frees the in-memory handle but leaves the file on disk untouched.

DetachResource.blurb: Removes a resource handle from the Resource Manager's bookkeeping so the caller owns it outright. Use when you want a long-lived copy you'll DisposeHandle yourself later.

ResError.blurb: Returns the result code of the last Resource Manager call. The way to tell why GetResource just returned NULL.

Get1Resource.signature: pascal Handle Get1Resource(ResType theType, short theID)
Get1Resource.blurb: Like GetResource but only searches the current resource file, not the chain. Use when you specifically want a resource from your own file, not a system one.

Count1Resources.blurb: Counts resources of a given type in the current resource file (1-based; pairs with GetIndResource).

NewRgn.blurb: Allocates an empty region. Free with DisposeRgn. Regions are arbitrary 2D shapes used for clipping, hit-testing, and complex window outlines.

DisposeRgn.blurb: Frees a region created with NewRgn / CopyRgn / etc.

RectRgn.signature: pascal void RectRgn(RgnHandle rgn, const Rect* r)
RectRgn.blurb: Sets a region's shape to a rectangle.

SetRectRgn.signature: pascal void SetRectRgn(RgnHandle rgn, short left, short top, short right, short bottom)
SetRectRgn.blurb: Sets a region's shape to a rectangle given by coordinates.

FrameRgn.blurb: Strokes a region's outline with the current pen pattern.

PaintRgn.blurb: Fills a region with the current pen pattern.

FillRgn.signature: pascal void FillRgn(RgnHandle rgn, const Pattern* pat)
FillRgn.blurb: Fills a region with the given pattern.

EraseRgn.blurb: Erases a region using the port's background pattern.

SetClip.blurb: Replaces the current port's clipping region. Subsequent drawing is masked to the intersection of the visible region and this one.

ClipRect.blurb: Sets the clipping region to a simple rectangle. Faster than building a full RgnHandle when you don't need a complex shape.

OpenPicture.blurb: Begins recording subsequent QuickDraw calls into a PICT. Pair with ClosePicture. The resulting PicHandle is the on-disk PICT format.

ClosePicture.blurb: Stops PICT recording. The PicHandle returned by the preceding OpenPicture now holds the complete picture.

DrawPicture.signature: pascal void DrawPicture(PicHandle myPicture, const Rect* dstRect)
DrawPicture.blurb: Renders a PICT into the current port, scaled to dstRect.

KillPicture.blurb: Disposes a PICT created with OpenPicture / GetPicture.

StopAlert.signature: pascal short StopAlert(short alertID, ModalFilterProcPtr filterProc)
StopAlert.blurb: Pops a stop-style alert (red stop sign icon) from an ALRT resource. Returns the item number of the button the user pressed.

NoteAlert.signature: pascal short NoteAlert(short alertID, ModalFilterProcPtr filterProc)

CautionAlert.signature: pascal short CautionAlert(short alertID, ModalFilterProcPtr filterProc)
CautionAlert.blurb: Pops a caution-style alert (yellow triangle icon). Use for "are you sure?" prompts.

Alert.signature: pascal short Alert(short alertID, ModalFilterProcPtr filterProc)
Alert.blurb: Pops a generic alert with no icon. The StopAlert / NoteAlert / CautionAlert variants are the friendlier wrappers.

ParamText.signature: pascal void ParamText(ConstStr255Param param0, ConstStr255Param param1, ConstStr255Param param2, ConstStr255Param param3)
ParamText.blurb: Sets the four parameter strings ^0..^3 that alert / dialog text substitutions reference. Always call right before Alert/StopAlert if your DITL text uses ^0/^1/^2/^3.

DialogSelect.signature: pascal Boolean DialogSelect(const EventRecord* theEvent, DialogPtr* theDialog, short* itemHit)
DialogSelect.blurb: Passes a modeless dialog event for the Dialog Manager to handle. Use in the main event loop when you have modeless dialogs alongside windows.

DialogPeek.blurb: A peek-into-the-record alias for DialogPtr. Lets you reach the window's textH / aDefItem fields when you need them.

DrawDialog.blurb: Forces a dialog to redraw its items. Usually called from an update event handler for the dialog window.

SetDialogItemText.signature: pascal void SetDialogItemText(Handle item, ConstStr255Param text)
SetDialogItemText.blurb: Sets the text of a static-text or edit-text item. The Handle comes from GetDialogItem's hItem output.

EnableItem.signature: pascal void EnableItem(MenuHandle menu, short item)
EnableItem.blurb: Enables a menu item so it appears black (not grey) and can be selected. Item 0 enables the menu title itself.

DisableItem.signature: pascal void DisableItem(MenuHandle menu, short item)
DisableItem.blurb: Greys out a menu item. Item 0 disables the menu title.

SetItemMark.signature: pascal void SetItemMark(MenuHandle menu, short item, short markChar)
SetItemMark.blurb: Sets a custom character to mark an item (vs CheckItem's checkmark). Pass `noMark` (0) to clear.

GetMenuBar.blurb: Returns a handle to the current menu bar. Useful if you want to swap menu bars in (e.g. before opening a dialog) and restore the old one later.

MoveWindow.signature: pascal void MoveWindow(WindowPtr w, short hGlobal, short vGlobal, Boolean front)
MoveWindow.blurb: Moves a window to (h, v) in global coordinates. Pass true for `front` to bring to front.

SizeWindow.signature: pascal void SizeWindow(WindowPtr w, short width, short height, Boolean fUpdate)
SizeWindow.blurb: Resizes the window's content area. fUpdate=true forces an update event for any newly-visible portion.

GrowWindow.signature: pascal long GrowWindow(WindowPtr w, Point startPt, const Rect* bBox)
GrowWindow.blurb: Tracks the size-box drag, returning the new width/height packed as `(height << 16) | width`. Call from FindWindow's `inGrow` branch then hand the result to SizeWindow.

ZoomWindow.signature: pascal void ZoomWindow(WindowPtr w, short partCode, Boolean front)
ZoomWindow.blurb: Toggles the window between user state and standard state. partCode is inZoomIn or inZoomOut from FindWindow.

GetPort.blurb: Reads the current port so you can restore it after a temporary SetPort. Common pattern: GetPort + SetPort + draw + SetPort(saved).

PenSize.signature: pascal void PenSize(short width, short height)
PenSize.blurb: Sets the pen's width and height in pixels. Default is 1x1.

PenNormal.blurb: Resets the pen to 1x1 size, black pattern, srcCopy mode. Use after temporary PenSize / PenPat / PenMode changes.

PenMode.blurb: Sets the pen's transfer mode (srcCopy, srcOr, srcXor, etc.). Affects how subsequent strokes combine with the destination.

PenPat.blurb: Sets the pen's fill pattern. Default is qd.black.

BlockMove.signature: pascal void BlockMove(const void* srcPtr, void* destPtr, Size byteCount)
BlockMove.blurb: Copies bytes between two non-relocatable blocks. Equivalent to memmove (handles overlap).

BlockMoveData.signature: pascal void BlockMoveData(const void* srcPtr, void* destPtr, Size byteCount)
BlockMoveData.blurb: Like BlockMove but skips the cache-flush — faster, safe for data that won't be executed.

MemError.blurb: Returns the result code of the last Memory Manager call. The way to find out why NewPtr / NewHandle just returned NULL.

FreeMem.blurb: Returns total free bytes in the application heap. Cheaper than calling MaxBlock.

MaxBlock.blurb: Returns the size of the largest contiguous free block in the application heap. Use to decide whether a big allocation can succeed.

HiWord.blurb: Returns the high 16 bits of a 32-bit value. Most commonly used to split MenuSelect's packed (menuID, item) result.

LoWord.blurb: Returns the low 16 bits of a 32-bit value. Pairs with HiWord.

Random.blurb: Returns a pseudo-random short in the range -32768..32767 (qd.randSeed holds state). For a non-negative N, use `(Random() & 0x7FFF) % N`.
